concepts.title: Diffraction of Light through a Single Slit
concepts.description: The intensity distribution of light in a single slit diffraction is given by the equation I = I0 * (sin(πa sinθ) / (πa sinθ))^2, where I0 is the maximum intensity, a is the width of the slit and θ is the angle of diffraction.

concepts.title: Interference of Light
concepts.description: Interference of light is the phenomenon where two or more light waves overlap to form a new pattern of light and dark bands. This is due to the superposition of light waves.

concepts.title: Conditions for Constructive and Destructive Interference
concepts.description: Constructive interference occurs when the phase difference between two waves is 0, 2π, 4π, ... (even multiples of 2π). Destructive interference occurs when the phase difference is π, 3π, 5π, ... (odd multiples of π).

concepts.title: Mnemonic for Types of Polarization
concepts.description: The mnemonic 'EVE' can be used to remember the types of polarization: E - Electric field, V - Vertical, E - Electric field.

concepts.title: Confusing Diffraction and Refraction
concepts.description: Students often confuse diffraction and refraction. Diffraction occurs due to the bending of light around an obstacle or through a narrow opening, while refraction occurs due to the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another.

concepts.title: Unit of Luminous Intensity
concepts.description: The unit of luminous intensity is candela (cd).

concepts.title: Michelson-Morley Experiment
concepts.description: The Michelson-Morley experiment was performed to detect the presence of an ether wind. The experiment showed that the speed of light is independent of the motion of the observer and the source of light.
